metadata.title: GCP Region Migration: europe-west4 to us-central1 (Iowa)
metadata.priority: P0 — GPU capacity exhausted in europe-west4 for 13+ hours, blocking all experiments
metadata.approach: Clean-slate migration: destroy europe-west4 Pulumi stack, recreate in us-central1. Same pattern as the successful europe-north1 to europe-west4 migration (2026-03-27). This is the SECOND region migration — lessons from the first are applied.
metadata.estimated-time: 3-4 hours total (Phase 0-6). Phase 7-8 are post-migration follow-up.
metadata.key-constraint: GCS bucket names are GLOBALLY UNIQUE. Pulumi will delete-then-create when region changes. Since all 3 GCS buckets are EMPTY (verified in FinOps snapshot), data loss is zero. Cloud SQL must be recreated (region is immutable). Current DB has experiment data from the europe-west4 Cloud Run MLflow — assess whether to export before destroying.
metadata.user-authorization: User explicitly authorized this migration due to 13+ hours GPU capacity exhaustion in europe-west4. No GDPR restriction — all datasets are public CC-BY mouse brain microscopy data. us-central1 has the highest GPU availability globally.

expert.assessment: The Pulumi code at deployment/pulumi/gcp/__main__.py is already parameterized: region = config.get("region") or "europe-west4". Changing region requires updating 3 files: Pulumi.yaml (default), Pulumi.dev.yaml (stack config), and __main__.py (default fallback + comments). Pulumi will delete-then-create ALL region-bound resources (GCS buckets, Cloud SQL, GAR, Cloud Run) because GCS location and Cloud SQL region are immutable attributes. force_destroy=True on buckets and deletion_protection=False on Cloud SQL make this safe. The enable_cloud_run flag is currently "true" in Pulumi.dev.yaml, so Cloud Run MLflow will be recreated too.
expert.recommendation: Modify existing stack (not blue-green). All 3 GCS buckets are empty per FinOps snapshot. Cloud SQL has experiment metadata but no irreplaceable data (all training artifacts were lost to the 413 bug). pulumi destroy then pulumi up with new region is the simplest path. Update GAR URL in all referencing files.
expert.risk: Pulumi destroy may timeout on Cloud SQL deletion (can take 5-10 min). Cloud Run service deletion is fast. GAR repo deletion may leave dangling image layers. Mitigation: use --yes flag and wait patiently.

expert.assessment:
Current data state (verified 2026-03-28 FinOps snapshot): - GCS buckets: ALL EMPTY (0 bytes across all 3 buckets) - GAR: 6.38 GB (base:latest 5.97 GiB + mlflow:v3.10.0 267 MiB) - Cloud SQL: Experiment metadata from Cloud Run MLflow sessions - DVC data: Must be re-pushed from local to new GCS bucket (2.89 GB from EBRAINS)
Docker images: Cannot use gcrane for cross-region copy because source GAR repo will be destroyed by Pulumi. Must re-push from local Docker cache. Verify local images exist before destroying old stack.
expert.recommendation: 1. Verify local Docker images exist: docker images | grep minivess-base 2. If local cache is missing, docker pull from europe-west4 GAR BEFORE destroy 3. After new GAR is created in us-central1, docker tag + docker push 4. Re-push DVC data to new GCS bucket via dvc push -r gcs 5. Total data transfer: ~6 GB images (upload) + ~3 GB DVC data (upload) = ~9 GB 6. Estimated time: 30-60 min on residential internet (10-20 Mbps upload)
expert.risk: If local Docker cache was pruned, base image must be rebuilt (45-60 min). Mitigation: check docker images BEFORE destroying old stack.

expert.assessment: This is a clean-slate migration with zero production traffic and zero users beyond the single researcher. Risk is low. The main concerns are: 1. Rollback complexity: once europe-west4 resources are destroyed, rollback means re-running the migration in reverse. Since europe-west4 is GPU-exhausted anyway, there is no rollback to a working state in that region. 2. DNS/URL changes: Cloud Run URL will change (region embedded in URL). All MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI references must be updated. 3. SkyPilot controller: currently in europe-west1-b. After migration, jobs target us-central1. Cross-region controller-to-job SSH will work but adds latency. Should move controller to us-central1 as well. 4. GCS bucket names can be reused since location is set at creation time and Pulumi destroy + create with same name in new region works (verified in first migration).
expert.recommendation:
Pre-migration checklist (verify before ANY destructive action): [ ] Docker images cached locally (docker images | grep minivess) [ ] DVC data cached locally (ls data/raw/minivess/imagesTr/ | wc -l) [ ] us-central1 GPU quotas verified (L4 + A100-80GB) [ ] us-central1 service availability verified (GAR, Cloud SQL, Cloud Run, GCS) [ ] Current .env backed up (cp .env .env.backup-ew4)
Post-migration verification: [ ] pulumi stack output shows us-central1 for all resources [ ] GAR image accessible from us-central1 VMs [ ] DVC data present in new GCS bucket [ ] Cloud Run MLflow responding (curl health check) [ ] SkyPilot can provision L4 in us-central1 (smoke test)
expert.risk: us-central1 could also become capacity-exhausted, though this is much less likely given its 4 zones and highest global GPU inventory. Long-term solution: multi-region config (already exists as europe_us.yaml) with co-located infra in both regions. For now, single-region in us-central1 is the pragmatic choice.

expert.assessment:
SkyPilot integration points that reference europe-west4: 1. .sky.yaml: controller infra: gcp/europe-west4 2. deployment/skypilot/train_factorial.yaml: image_id docker:europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev/... 3. deployment/skypilot/train_production.yaml: same image_id pattern 4. deployment/skypilot/train_hpo.yaml: same 5. deployment/skypilot/hpo_grid_worker.yaml: same + explicit region field 6. deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gcp.yaml: same 7. configs/cloud/gcp_spot.yaml: region + docker_image fields 8. configs/cloud/regions/europe_strict.yaml: region: europe-west4 9. configs/cloud/regions/europe_a100_fallback.yaml: region: europe-west4 10. configs/cloud/regions/europe_us.yaml: europe-west4 as first priority 11. configs/cloud/regions/europe.yaml: europe-west4 references
Controller placement: the current controller (europe-west1-b, n4-standard-4) must be stopped. The .sky.yaml must be updated to target us-central1. SkyPilot will auto-provision a new controller in us-central1 when the next job is submitted.
IMPORTANT: The old controller VM will NOT be auto-deleted. Must manually stop it via sky jobs controller stop and verify the GCE instance is terminated.
expert.recommendation: 1. Stop old controller: sky jobs controller stop (frees EUR 3.22/day) 2. Update .sky.yaml: infra: gcp/us-central1 3. Update ALL SkyPilot YAMLs: image_id to us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/... 4. Create us_central1_strict.yaml for single-region zero-egress config 5. Update europe_us.yaml to put us-central1 FIRST (since infra is now there) 6. Verify quota: sky show-gpus L4 --cloud gcp (check us-central1 availability) 7. Run smoke test before factorial launch

expert.assessment:
Test files referencing europe-west4 (from previous migration, grep results): - tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_skypilot_yamls.py - tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_docker_image_consistency.py - tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_preflight_mocked.py - tests/v2/unit/test_docker_pull_optimization.py - tests/v2/unit/finops/test_cost_governance.py (if it exists from 11th pass) - tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_yaml_contract_enforcement.py
The previous migration plan noted a DESIGN IMPROVEMENT: tests should read region from configs/registry/gar.yaml (single source of truth) instead of hardcoding. If this was implemented, many tests will auto-adapt. If not, we need to update all hardcoded europe-west4 assertions to us-central1.
Post-migration test gate: make test-staging must pass with 0 failures. New tests to add: region consistency tests (all config files reference same region).
expert.recommendation: 1. Run grep for "europe-west4" across entire codebase to find all references 2. Batch-update all references to us-central1 3. Run make test-staging immediately after code changes 4. Add T8 cloud test suite improvements for future migrations

consensus.decision: Clean-slate migration: pulumi destroy (europe-west4), pulumi up (us-central1).
consensus.rationale: All 5 experts agree: zero data at risk (GCS empty, Cloud SQL expendable), Pulumi code is already parameterized, Docker images re-pushable from local cache. The second migration benefits from the first migration's lessons learned. Estimated wall-clock: 3-4 hours including Docker image upload time.
consensus.lessons-from-first-migration: 1. DATA NOT MIGRATED: GCS buckets were created empty — DVC data was not copied. Prevention: Push DVC data to new GCS bucket immediately after Pulumi up. 2. DVC CACHE LOST: Had to re-download from EBRAINS. Prevention: Verify local DVC cache exists before starting. 3. STALE SKYPILOT PATHS: API server had stale region paths. Prevention: sky api stop and sky api start after migration. 4. DOCKER IMAGE FRESHNESS: Image needed rebuild. Prevention: Verify local Docker cache before destroying old GAR.

phase.name: Pre-Migration Verification
phase.description: Verify us-central1 readiness and local cache state before any destructive action. NOTHING is modified in this phase. All actions are read-only verification.

task.title: Verify us-central1 GPU quotas (L4 + A100-80GB)
task.commands:
# Check L4 GPU quota in us-central1 gcloud compute regions describe us-central1 --project=minivess-mlops \ --format="table(quotas.filter('metric:NVIDIA_L4_GPUS').extract(limit,usage))"
# Check A100-80GB GPU quota in us-central1 gcloud compute regions describe us-central1 --project=minivess-mlops \ --format="table(quotas.filter('metric:NVIDIA_A100_80GB_GPUS').extract(limit,usage))"
# Check general CPU quota (needed for controller + training VMs) gcloud compute regions describe us-central1 --project=minivess-mlops \ --format="table(quotas.filter('metric:CPUS').extract(limit,usage))"
# Verify SkyPilot can see L4 in us-central1 uv run sky show-gpus L4 --cloud gcp 2>&1 | grep us-central1
task.verification: L4 quota >= 1 in us-central1. CPU quota >= 8 (controller 2 vCPU + training VM 4+ vCPU). sky show-gpus shows us-central1 with L4 availability.
task.rollback: No changes made. If quota is 0, request increase via GCP Console: https://console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/quotas?project=minivess-mlops Filter by region=us-central1, metric contains "GPU". Request L4 quota >= 1 and GPUS_ALL_REGIONS >= 1.
task.failure-action: STOP entire migration. If us-central1 has zero GPU quota, the migration will not help. Request quota increase (takes 24-48h for L4, sometimes instant). Alternative: check us-east4 or us-west1 as backup targets.

task.title: Verify us-central1 service availability (GAR, Cloud SQL, Cloud Run, GCS)
task.commands:
# Verify Artifact Registry is available in us-central1 gcloud artifacts locations list --format="value(name)" | grep us-central1
# Verify Cloud SQL supports us-central1 gcloud sql tiers list --format="table(tier,region)" | grep us-central1 | head -3
# Verify Cloud Run supports us-central1 gcloud run regions list --format="value(name)" | grep us-central1
# Verify GCS supports us-central1 (GCS supports all regions) echo "GCS supports all regions — no verification needed."
task.verification: us-central1 appears in GAR locations, Cloud SQL tiers, and Cloud Run regions.
task.rollback: No changes made. If any service is unavailable, choose alternative US region.

task.title: Verify local Docker image cache exists
task.commands:
# Check if base image is in local Docker cache docker images | grep minivess-base docker images | grep "minivess.*mlflow"
# If images are NOT found locally, pull from europe-west4 GAR BEFORE destroying: # docker pull europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/base:latest # docker pull europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/mlflow:v3.10.0 # docker pull europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/mlflow-gcp:latest
task.verification: docker images shows minivess-base:latest (~6 GB) and mlflow images. If not found, pull them from europe-west4 GAR before proceeding to Phase 1.
task.rollback: No changes made.
task.failure-action: If images not in local cache AND europe-west4 GAR is inaccessible: Must rebuild from source (deployment/docker/Dockerfile.base). Takes 45-60 min. Proceed to Phase 1 after rebuild completes.

task.title: Verify local DVC data cache exists
task.commands:
# Check local MiniVess data ls data/raw/minivess/imagesTr/ 2>/dev/null | wc -l # Expected: 70 files (70 volumes)
# Check local DeepVess data (if available) ls data/raw/deepvess/images/ 2>/dev/null | wc -l
# Check DVC tracking status dvc status
task.verification: At least 70 files in data/raw/minivess/imagesTr/. DVC data is locally cached and ready for push to new GCS bucket.
task.rollback: No changes made.
task.failure-action: If local DVC data is missing, must re-download from EBRAINS: python -m minivess.data.acquisition --dataset minivess Takes ~15-30 min depending on EBRAINS bandwidth.

task.title: Snapshot current europe-west4 state for reference
task.commands:
# Capture current Pulumi outputs for reference cd deployment/pulumi/gcp && pulumi stack output 2>&1 | tee /tmp/pulumi-ew4-outputs.txt && cd -
# Capture current .env for rollback cp .env .env.backup-europe-west4 2>/dev/null || echo "No .env file to backup"
# Record current Cloud Run URL gcloud run services describe minivess-mlflow --region=europe-west4 --project=minivess-mlops \ --format="value(status.url)" 2>/dev/null || echo "Cloud Run not found or not accessible"
# Record current Cloud SQL IP gcloud sql instances describe mlflow-db-479b793 --project=minivess-mlops \ --format="value(ipAddresses[0].ipAddress)" 2>/dev/null || echo "Cloud SQL not found"
task.verification: Pulumi outputs captured to /tmp/pulumi-ew4-outputs.txt. .env backup created at .env.backup-europe-west4.
task.rollback: No changes made. This IS the rollback preparation.

task.title: Cancel stuck europe-west4 SkyPilot jobs
task.commands:
# Check current job status uv run sky jobs queue
# Cancel all PENDING jobs (they will never provision in europe-west4) # Replace job IDs with actual PENDING job IDs from sky jobs queue output: uv run sky jobs cancel 165 uv run sky jobs cancel 166 # Cancel any other PENDING jobs listed
# Verify all jobs cancelled uv run sky jobs queue
task.verification: No PENDING or RUNNING jobs remain in the queue.
task.rollback: Jobs were already stuck PENDING — cancelling them is non-destructive.

phase.name: Pulumi Infrastructure Migration
phase.description: Update Pulumi config to us-central1, destroy old stack, deploy new stack. Clean-slate approach: no data migration needed (GCS buckets are empty).

task.title: Update Pulumi configuration files for us-central1
task.changes:
# deployment/pulumi/gcp/__main__.py: # Line 3: "europe-west4 (Netherlands)" -> "us-central1 (Iowa)" # Line 30: 'or "europe-west4"' -> 'or "us-central1"' # Line 144: comment "europe-west4" -> "us-central1" # Line 155: comment "europe-west4" -> "us-central1"
# deployment/pulumi/gcp/Pulumi.yaml: # Line 11: description "europe-west4" -> "us-central1" # Line 18: default: europe-west4 -> default: us-central1 # Line 19: description "europe-west4 = Netherlands" -> "us-central1 = Iowa"
# deployment/pulumi/gcp/Pulumi.dev.yaml: # Line 3: gcp:region: europe-west4 -> gcp:region: us-central1
task.verification: cd deployment/pulumi/gcp grep -n "us-central1" __main__.py Pulumi.yaml Pulumi.dev.yaml # Should show us-central1 in all config locations grep -n "europe-west4" __main__.py Pulumi.yaml Pulumi.dev.yaml # Should show ZERO matches (except possibly in historical comments)
task.rollback: git checkout -- deployment/pulumi/gcp/__main__.py git checkout -- deployment/pulumi/gcp/Pulumi.yaml git checkout -- deployment/pulumi/gcp/Pulumi.dev.yaml

task.title: Run pulumi preview to see planned changes
task.commands: cd deployment/pulumi/gcp pulumi preview --diff 2>&1 | tee /tmp/pulumi-preview-usc1.txt # Expected: ALL region-bound resources show "replace" (delete + create): # - GCS buckets: replace (location change) # - Cloud SQL: replace (region change) # - GAR repos: replace (location change) # - Cloud Run: replace (location change) # - IAM: no change (global)
task.verification: Preview shows "replace" for GCS, Cloud SQL, GAR, Cloud Run resources. NO unexpected deletions of global resources (IAM, project settings). Total resource count matches current stack.
task.rollback: Preview is read-only. No changes made.

task.title: Stop SkyPilot controller and destroy old Pulumi stack
task.commands:
# Stop SkyPilot controller (frees the VM in europe-west1-b) uv run sky jobs controller stop --yes
# Verify controller is stopped gcloud compute instances list --project=minivess-mlops \ --format="table(name,zone,machineType,status)" | grep sky-jobs
# Destroy old europe-west4 Pulumi stack cd deployment/pulumi/gcp pulumi destroy --yes
# Verify: no resources remain pulumi stack export | python3 -c " import sys, json d = json.load(sys.stdin) resources = d.get('deployment', {}).get('resources', []) print(f'{len(resources)} resources remaining (should be 1 — the stack itself)') "
task.verification: Controller VM is TERMINATED or not listed. pulumi stack export shows 1 resource (the stack itself). gcloud artifacts repositories list shows no europe-west4 repos. gcloud sql instances list shows no instances. gcloud run services list shows no services.
task.rollback: If pulumi destroy fails partway: 1. Check which resources remain: pulumi stack export 2. Delete manually via gcloud if needed 3. Then run pulumi refresh to sync state
task.warning: This DELETES all europe-west4 resources including Cloud SQL with experiment metadata. Verified acceptable: GCS buckets are empty, experiment data is expendable for a greenfield project.
task.estimated-time: 10-15 minutes (Cloud SQL deletion can take 5-10 min)

task.title: Deploy new Pulumi stack in us-central1
task.commands:
cd deployment/pulumi/gcp pulumi up --yes
# This creates in us-central1: # - GCS buckets: minivess-mlops-dvc-data, minivess-mlops-mlflow-artifacts, minivess-mlops-checkpoints # - Cloud SQL: mlflow-db (PostgreSQL 15, db-g1-small) # - GAR: minivess (STANDARD), docker-hub-cache (REMOTE) # - Cloud Run: minivess-mlflow (if enable_cloud_run=true) # - IAM: skypilot-training SA, mlflow-server SA
task.verification:
# Verify all outputs point to us-central1 pulumi stack output 2>&1 | tee /tmp/pulumi-usc1-outputs.txt
# Expected outputs: # docker_registry: us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess # region: us-central1 # mlflow_url: https://minivess-mlflow-*.run.app (us-central1)
# Verify GCS buckets exist in US-CENTRAL1 gsutil ls -L -b gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data/ 2>&1 | grep "Location" # Expected: US-CENTRAL1
gsutil ls -L -b gs://minivess-mlops-mlflow-artifacts/ 2>&1 | grep "Location" gsutil ls -L -b gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints/ 2>&1 | grep "Location"
# Verify GAR repository gcloud artifacts repositories list --project=minivess-mlops \ --format="table(name,format,mode)" --location=us-central1
# Verify Cloud SQL gcloud sql instances list --project=minivess-mlops \ --format="table(name,region,state,tier)"
# Verify Cloud Run (if enabled) gcloud run services list --project=minivess-mlops \ --format="table(name,region,url)"
task.rollback: If pulumi up fails: 1. Read error message — likely quota or API issue 2. Fix the issue (enable API, request quota) 3. Re-run pulumi up --yes If partial deployment, pulumi destroy --yes and start over

task.title: Push Docker images to us-central1 GAR
task.commands:
# Authenticate to new GAR region gcloud auth configure-docker us-central1-docker.pkg.dev
# Tag and push base image docker tag minivess-base:latest \ us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/base:latest docker push us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/base:latest
# Tag and push MLflow image (for Cloud Run) docker tag minivess-mlflow:latest \ us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/mlflow:v3.10.0 docker push us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/mlflow:v3.10.0
# Tag and push MLflow GCP image (for Cloud Run with GCS) docker tag minivess-mlflow-gcp:latest \ us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/mlflow-gcp:latest docker push us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/mlflow-gcp:latest
task.verification:
# Verify images in us-central1 GAR gcloud artifacts docker images list \ us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess \ --include-tags --format="table(package,tags,createTime)"
# Expected: base:latest, mlflow:v3.10.0, mlflow-gcp:latest
task.rollback: If push fails (auth error): gcloud auth configure-docker us-central1-docker.pkg.dev If push fails (quota): check GAR storage quota in us-central1 If local image missing: rebuild from source (see T0.3 failure-action)
task.note: Upload time: ~30-60 min for 6.4 GB base image on residential internet. MLflow images are small (~267 MiB each, ~2-3 min). This is the LONGEST task in the migration. Start it and proceed to T1.6 in parallel.

task.title: Push DVC data to us-central1 GCS bucket
task.commands:
# Configure DVC remote to point to new GCS bucket (same name, new region) dvc remote modify gcs url gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data
# Push MiniVess data to new GCS bucket dvc push data/raw/minivess -r gcs
# Push DeepVess data if available locally dvc push data/raw/deepvess.dvc -r gcs 2>/dev/null || echo "DeepVess not in local DVC cache"
# Verify data on GCS gsutil du -s gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data/
task.verification: gsutil du -s shows ~2-3 GB in gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data/. dvc status -r gcs shows no untracked changes.
task.rollback: DVC push is idempotent. If it fails, fix the issue and re-run. If local data is missing, re-download from EBRAINS.
task.note: Can run in PARALLEL with T1.5 (Docker push) since they use different upload paths. DVC data: ~2.89 GB, estimated 10-20 min on residential internet.

task.title: Verify Cloud Run MLflow is healthy in us-central1
task.commands:
# Get the new Cloud Run URL from Pulumi output MLFLOW_URL=$(cd deployment/pulumi/gcp && pulumi stack output mlflow_url && cd -)
# Health check — MLflow should respond curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "${MLFLOW_URL}/health" || \ curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "${MLFLOW_URL}/api/2.0/mlflow/experiments/search?max_results=1"
# Test MLflow client connectivity python3 -c " import mlflow import os os.environ['MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI'] = '${MLFLOW_URL}' client = mlflow.tracking.MlflowClient() exps = client.search_experiments(max_results=1) print(f'MLflow OK: {len(exps)} experiments') "
task.verification: HTTP 200 from Cloud Run MLflow health endpoint. MLflow client can connect and list experiments.
task.rollback: If Cloud Run not responding: 1. Check Cloud Run logs: gcloud run services logs read minivess-mlflow --region us-central1 2. Verify Cloud SQL IP is accessible from Cloud Run (check authorized networks) 3. Verify MLflow image was pushed correctly to us-central1 GAR
task.depends-on: T1.4 (Pulumi stack deployed), T1.5 (MLflow image pushed to GAR)

phase.name: Configuration Updates — All Region References
phase.description: Update ALL files referencing europe-west4 to us-central1. Single atomic commit after all changes are made and tests pass. Expert E5 (Test Engineer) identified the full file list.

task.title: Update .env and .env.example — GCP region and MLflow URL
task.changes:
# .env.example: # Line 417: GCP_REGION=europe-west4 -> GCP_REGION=us-central1
# .env (actual values): # GCP_REGION=us-central1 # MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=<new Cloud Run URL from pulumi stack output mlflow_url>
task.verification: grep GCP_REGION .env.example # Should show us-central1 grep GCP_REGION .env # Should show us-central1 grep MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI .env # Should show new Cloud Run URL
task.rollback: cp .env.backup-europe-west4 .env

task.title: Update configs/registry/gar.yaml — GAR server URL
task.changes: # configs/registry/gar.yaml: # server: europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev -> server: us-central1-docker.pkg.dev # image_prefix: europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev/... -> image_prefix: us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/...
task.verification: grep "us-central1" configs/registry/gar.yaml # Should match grep "europe-west4" configs/registry/gar.yaml # Should NOT match
task.rollback: git checkout -- configs/registry/gar.yaml

task.title: Update configs/cloud/gcp_spot.yaml — region and docker_image
task.changes: # configs/cloud/gcp_spot.yaml: # Line 2: comment "europe-west4" -> "us-central1" # Line 8: region: europe-west4 -> region: us-central1 # Line 24: docker_image: europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev/... -> us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/...
task.verification: grep "region:" configs/cloud/gcp_spot.yaml # Should show us-central1 grep "docker_image:" configs/cloud/gcp_spot.yaml # Should show us-central1
task.rollback: git checkout -- configs/cloud/gcp_spot.yaml

task.title: Create configs/cloud/regions/us_central1_strict.yaml
task.content:
# Single-region us-central1 — same region as GAR, GCS, Cloud SQL. # Zero cross-region egress. Lowest latency to all GCP infrastructure. # # Use for debug runs where cost predictability matters more than availability. # us-central1 has 3 L4 zones + 4 A100 zones — highest GPU availability globally.
description: "Single-region us-central1 — zero cross-region egress"
regions: gcp: L4: - region: us-central1 # Iowa — 3 zones, co-located with GAR/GCS zones: 3
task.verification: cat configs/cloud/regions/us_central1_strict.yaml # Should be valid YAML python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('configs/cloud/regions/us_central1_strict.yaml'))"
task.rollback: rm configs/cloud/regions/us_central1_strict.yaml

task.title: Create configs/cloud/regions/us_central1_a100_fallback.yaml
task.content:
# US regions with A100 fallback — use when L4 spot is capacity-exhausted. # # Priority order (enforced by region_injection.py iteration order): # 1. L4 in us-central1 — cheapest ($0.224/hr spot), co-located with GAR/GCS # 2. A100 in us-central1 — fallback ($1.10/hr spot), same region as infra # # User authorization: 2026-03-28 (region migration to us-central1) # Cost impact: A100 debug pass = ~$18.70 vs L4 at ~$6.85 (+$11.85) # # See: docs/planning/v0-2_archive/original_docs/gpu-instances-finops-report.md
description: "US regions with A100 fallback — L4 primary, A100 when L4 exhausted"
regions: gcp: L4: - region: us-central1 # Iowa — co-located with GAR/GCS zones: 3 A100-80GB: - region: us-central1 # Iowa — same region as infra (PREEMPTIBLE ONLY, quota 1) zones: 3
task.verification: python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('configs/cloud/regions/us_central1_a100_fallback.yaml'))"
task.rollback: rm configs/cloud/regions/us_central1_a100_fallback.yaml

task.title: Update configs/factorial/debug.yaml — region_config
task.verification: grep "region_config:" configs/factorial/debug.yaml # Should show: us_central1_a100_fallback
task.rollback: git checkout -- configs/factorial/debug.yaml

task.title: Update SkyPilot YAMLs — GAR image_id to us-central1
task.changes:
# ALL SkyPilot YAMLs: # image_id: docker:europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/base:latest # -> # image_id: docker:us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/base:latest
# hpo_grid_worker.yaml ALSO: # region: europe-west4 -> region: us-central1 (if explicit region field exists)
# ALL: update comments referencing europe-west4 to us-central1
task.verification:
# Verify ALL SkyPilot YAMLs reference us-central1 GAR grep -r "europe-west4" deployment/skypilot/*.yaml # Should return ZERO matches
grep -r "us-central1" deployment/skypilot/*.yaml # Should show image_id lines for all files
task.rollback: git checkout -- deployment/skypilot/

task.title: Update .sky.yaml — controller placement to us-central1
task.verification: grep "infra:" .sky.yaml # Should show gcp/us-central1 grep "europe-west4" .sky.yaml # Should return ZERO matches
task.rollback: git checkout -- .sky.yaml

task.title: Update scripts/preflight_gcp.py — GAR image URL
task.verification: grep "GAR_IMAGE" scripts/preflight_gcp.py # Should show us-central1
task.rollback: git checkout -- scripts/preflight_gcp.py

task.title: Update region config files — europe_strict, europe_a100_fallback, europe_us
task.changes:
# europe_strict.yaml: # Update comments to note "LEGACY — infra has moved to us-central1" # Keep the file for reference but it's no longer the co-located config
# europe_a100_fallback.yaml: # Update comments to note "LEGACY — infra has moved to us-central1"
# europe_us.yaml: # Move us-central1 to FIRST position (since that's where infra lives now) # Update comment: "closest to GCS us-central1"
# europe.yaml: # Update comments referencing "GAR/GCS in europe-west4"
task.verification: # Verify us_central1_strict.yaml is the new default co-located config cat configs/cloud/regions/us_central1_strict.yaml
task.rollback: git checkout -- configs/cloud/regions/

task.title: Update governance and documentation files
task.changes:
# CLAUDE.md: # All references to "europe-west4 (Netherlands)" -> "us-central1 (Iowa)" # GCP_REGION, docker registry URLs, Cloud Run references
# deployment/pulumi/gcp/CLAUDE.md: # Region: europe-west4 -> us-central1 # GAR URL: europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev -> us-central1-docker.pkg.dev # Prerequisites: GPU quota region
# .claude/rules/two-providers-only.md: # region: europe-west4 -> region: us-central1
# .claude/rules/data-on-gcs.md: # No region references (bucket names are region-agnostic) — verify
# knowledge-graph/domains/cloud.yaml: # Line 130: region: europe-west4 -> region: us-central1 # Line 163: url: europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev/... -> us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/...
task.verification:
# Comprehensive grep for stale europe-west4 references grep -r "europe-west4" \ CLAUDE.md \ deployment/pulumi/gcp/CLAUDE.md \ .claude/rules/ \ knowledge-graph/domains/cloud.yaml \ configs/ \ deployment/skypilot/ \ scripts/preflight_gcp.py \ .sky.yaml \ .env.example
# Should return ZERO matches (except historical/archived docs)
task.rollback: git checkout -- CLAUDE.md deployment/pulumi/gcp/CLAUDE.md .claude/rules/ knowledge-graph/domains/cloud.yaml

task.title: Update test files referencing europe-west4
task.commands:
# Find ALL test files referencing europe-west4 grep -rl "europe-west4" tests/ 2>/dev/null
# Update each file: replace europe-west4 with us-central1 # Files likely affected (from previous migration experience): # tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_skypilot_yamls.py # tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_docker_image_consistency.py # tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_preflight_mocked.py # tests/v2/unit/test_docker_pull_optimization.py # tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_yaml_contract_enforcement.py # tests/v2/unit/finops/test_cost_governance.py
# DESIGN IMPROVEMENT (from 11th pass plan): # Tests that read from configs/registry/gar.yaml will auto-adapt. # Tests with hardcoded "europe-west4" assertions must be updated to read from config.
task.verification: grep -r "europe-west4" tests/ # Should return ZERO matches grep -r "us-central1" tests/ # Should show updated assertions
task.rollback: git checkout -- tests/

phase.name: SkyPilot Setup
phase.description: Configure SkyPilot for us-central1. Restart API server to clear stale paths. Verify controller auto-provisions in us-central1 on next job launch.

task.title: Restart SkyPilot API server (clear stale region state)
task.commands:
# Lesson from first migration: SkyPilot API server caches stale region paths. # Must restart to pick up new .sky.yaml config. uv run sky api stop uv run sky api start
# Verify API server version uv run sky api status
task.verification: SkyPilot API server is running with current client version. No version mismatch warning.
task.rollback: If API server won't start: sky api stop --force, then sky api start

task.title: Verify SkyPilot GCP backend is configured
task.commands:
# Verify GCP is enabled in SkyPilot uv run sky check gcp
# Verify us-central1 L4 GPU availability uv run sky show-gpus L4 --cloud gcp 2>&1 | head -20
# Verify .sky.yaml is loaded correctly uv run sky api status cat .sky.yaml
task.verification: sky check gcp shows GCP as enabled. sky show-gpus L4 shows us-central1 with availability. .sky.yaml shows infra: gcp/us-central1.
task.rollback: If GCP not enabled: gcloud auth application-default login Then: uv run sky check gcp

task.title: Verify old controller VM is terminated
task.commands:
# Check for any lingering controller VMs gcloud compute instances list --project=minivess-mlops \ --format="table(name,zone,machineType,status)" | grep sky-jobs
# If old controller still exists: # gcloud compute instances delete <name> --zone=europe-west1-b --project=minivess-mlops --quiet
task.verification: No sky-jobs-controller instances in europe-west1-b or europe-west4. Only resources should be those created by Pulumi in us-central1.
task.rollback: N/A — the old controller is waste. Deletion is always correct.

phase.name: Verification — Full Test Suite + Smoke Test
phase.description: Run all verification gates before launching any experiments. This phase catches any stale references or misconfiguration.

task.title: Run make test-staging — all tests must pass
task.commands: make test-staging
task.verification: All tests pass. 0 failures. Minimal skips (only hardware-gated acceptable). No europe-west4 assertion failures.
task.rollback: If tests fail: 1. Run with --maxfail=200 (not -x) to see ALL failures 2. Group by root cause (likely stale region references) 3. Batch-fix all instances of each root cause 4. Re-run make test-staging

task.title: Run scripts/preflight_gcp.py — all checks must pass
task.commands: uv run python scripts/preflight_gcp.py
task.verification: All preflight checks pass (15/15 or whatever the current count is). Docker image found on us-central1 GAR. GCS bucket accessible. DVC data present.
task.rollback: If Docker image check fails: verify T1.5 completed successfully. If GCS bucket check fails: verify T1.4 completed successfully. If DVC data check fails: verify T1.6 completed successfully.

task.title: Run validate_yaml_contract.py — YAML contract compliance
task.commands: uv run python scripts/validate_yaml_contract.py
task.verification: All SkyPilot YAMLs pass contract validation. No unauthorized GPU types, cloud providers, or resource configurations.
task.rollback: Fix any contract violations found. Most likely: stale region in YAML.

task.title: Dry-run: run_factorial.sh --dry-run
task.commands: ./scripts/run_factorial.sh --dry-run configs/factorial/debug.yaml
task.verification: Dry run prints all job launch commands. All image_id references show us-central1-docker.pkg.dev. Region injection uses us_central1_a100_fallback or us_central1_strict config. No europe-west4 references in any generated command.
task.rollback: Fix any stale references in configs/factorial/debug.yaml or run_factorial.sh.

task.title: Verify MLflow Cloud Run connectivity from Python
task.commands:
# Load .env and test MLflow connectivity set -a; source .env; set +a
python3 -c " import os import mlflow
uri = os.environ['MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI'] print(f'Testing MLflow at: {uri}') client = mlflow.tracking.MlflowClient(tracking_uri=uri) exps = client.search_experiments(max_results=5) print(f'MLflow OK: {len(exps)} experiments found') print(f'Artifact root: {exps[0].artifact_location if exps else \"no experiments\"}') "
task.verification: MLflow client connects successfully. Artifact root points to gs://minivess-mlops-mlflow-artifacts.
task.rollback: If connection fails: 1. Verify MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI in .env matches pulumi stack output mlflow_url 2. Verify Cloud Run service is healthy: gcloud run services describe minivess-mlflow --region us-central1 3. Check Cloud SQL connectivity from Cloud Run

task.title: Verify DVC pull from us-central1 GCS bucket
task.commands:
# Verify DVC remote points to correct bucket dvc remote list dvc remote default
# Test DVC status (should show "OK" or "up to date") dvc status -r gcs
# Verify data count in GCS gsutil ls gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data/ | head -20 gsutil du -s gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data/
task.verification: DVC remote 'gcs' points to gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data. gsutil du shows >0 bytes (data was pushed in T1.6).
task.rollback: If DVC data missing: re-run T1.6 (dvc push -r gcs). If bucket is wrong: dvc remote modify gcs url gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data

task.title: Comprehensive stale-reference grep
task.commands:
# Search for ANY remaining europe-west4 references in active code/config # Exclude: docs/planning/ (archived plans), .claude/metalearning/ (historical), # node_modules, .git, __pycache__ grep -r "europe-west4" \ --include="*.py" --include="*.yaml" --include="*.yml" --include="*.sh" \ --include="*.md" --include="*.toml" --include="*.json" --include="*.env*" \ --exclude-dir=".git" --exclude-dir="__pycache__" --exclude-dir="node_modules" \ --exclude-dir="docs/planning/v0-2_archive" \ --exclude-dir=".claude/metalearning" \ . 2>/dev/null | grep -v "Binary file" | sort
# For active config/code files, this should return ZERO results. # Historical docs (metalearning, archived plans) are OK to keep old references.
task.verification: Zero matches in active code, config, and deployment files. Only matches in archived/historical documentation (acceptable).
task.rollback: Fix any stale references found.

phase.name: Launch — Smoke Test in us-central1
phase.description: Launch a single smoke test job in us-central1 to verify end-to-end: Docker pull, DVC data access, CUDA availability, MLflow connectivity. Only after smoke test succeeds, proceed with factorial experiment.

task.title: Launch DynUNet smoke test in us-central1
task.commands: cd deployment/skypilot uv run sky jobs launch train_factorial.yaml \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=dynunet \ --env LOSS_NAME=dice_ce \ --env WITH_AUX_CALIB=false \ --env FOLD_ID=0 \ --env MAX_EPOCHS=2 \ --env MAX_TRAIN_VOLUMES=23 \ --env MAX_VAL_VOLUMES=12 \ --env EXPERIMENT_NAME=test_us_central1 \ --env-file ../../.env \ -y
task.verification:
# Monitor job status uv run sky jobs queue
# Expected progression: PENDING -> STARTING -> RUNNING -> SUCCEEDED # In us-central1, PENDING -> RUNNING should take < 5 min (high GPU availability)
# Verify job is in us-central1 uv run sky jobs queue --format json | python3 -c " import sys, json jobs = json.load(sys.stdin) for j in jobs: print(f'Job {j[\"id\"]}: {j[\"status\"]} region={j.get(\"region\", \"unknown\")}') "
# After job reaches RUNNING, verify in GCE: gcloud compute instances list --project=minivess-mlops \ --filter="zone:us-central1-*" --format="table(name,machineType,zone,status)"
task.rollback: If job fails: 1. Check logs: uv run sky jobs logs 2. Common failures: - Docker pull error: verify GAR image exists in us-central1 - DVC pull error: verify data in GCS bucket - CUDA error: GPU quota issue - MLflow error: verify Cloud Run URL in .env 3. Fix and re-launch

task.title: Verify controller provisioned in us-central1
task.commands:
# After first job launch, SkyPilot auto-provisions controller in us-central1 # Verify controller placement gcloud compute instances list --project=minivess-mlops \ --format="table(name,zone,machineType,status)" | grep sky-jobs
# Expected: controller in us-central1-* zone (a, b, or c) # Expected: machine type e2-medium or similar (from .sky.yaml cpus: 2+, memory: 4+)
task.verification: Controller VM in us-central1 (not europe-west1 or europe-west4). Machine type matches .sky.yaml spec (cpus: 2+, memory: 4+).
task.rollback: If controller not in us-central1: 1. Verify .sky.yaml has infra: gcp/us-central1 2. sky jobs controller stop --yes 3. Re-launch job (controller will re-provision)

task.title: Verify smoke test Docker pull time (same-region validation)
task.commands:
# Check job logs for Docker pull timing uv run sky jobs logs <JOB_ID> 2>&1 | grep -i "pull\|docker\|image" | head -20
# Same-region Docker pull should complete in < 60 seconds (6.4 GB) # Cross-region (europe -> US) would take 5-10 minutes
task.verification: Docker pull time < 60 seconds (confirms same-region pull from us-central1 GAR). No cross-region egress charges (verify in billing console next day).
task.rollback: N/A — this is a verification step.

phase.name: Factorial Experiment Launch
phase.description: After smoke test succeeds in us-central1, launch the full factorial experiment. This is the actual work that was blocked by europe-west4 GPU exhaustion.
phase.estimated-time: 10 minutes (launch time; experiments run for hours)

task.title: Launch Phase 1 validation jobs in us-central1
task.commands:
# Launch the factorial experiment using the debug config ./scripts/run_factorial.sh configs/factorial/debug.yaml
# Or launch individual Phase 1 validation jobs: cd deployment/skypilot for condition in \ "MODEL_FAMILY=dynunet LOSS_NAME=dice_ce WITH_AUX_CALIB=false FOLD_ID=0" \ "MODEL_FAMILY=dynunet LOSS_NAME=cbdice_cldice WITH_AUX_CALIB=false FOLD_ID=0" \ "MODEL_FAMILY=mambavesselnet LOSS_NAME=dice_ce WITH_AUX_CALIB=false FOLD_ID=0" do uv run sky jobs launch train_factorial.yaml \ --env ${condition} \ --env MAX_EPOCHS=2 \ --env MAX_TRAIN_VOLUMES=23 \ --env MAX_VAL_VOLUMES=12 \ --env EXPERIMENT_NAME=debug_factorial \ --env-file ../../.env \ -y & sleep 5 # Rate limit between submissions done wait
task.verification:
# Monitor all jobs uv run sky jobs queue
# In us-central1, jobs should transition from PENDING to RUNNING within minutes # (not 13+ hours like in europe-west4)
# Expected: all 3 jobs RUNNING within 10 minutes of submission
task.rollback: If jobs still PENDING after 30 min in us-central1: 1. Check quota: gcloud compute regions describe us-central1 --project=minivess-mlops 2. Check if it's a spot capacity issue: try --no-use-spot for one job 3. If us-central1 is also exhausted: fall back to us-east4 or us-west1
task.estimated-time: 10 minutes to launch; experiments run for 1-3 hours each

task.title: Monitor initial provisioning (first 30 min)
task.commands: # Poll every 60 seconds for 30 minutes for i in $(seq 1 30); do echo "=== Check $i ($(date -u +%H:%M:%S)) ===" uv run sky jobs queue --format json | python3 -c " import sys, json jobs = json.load(sys.stdin) for j in jobs: print(f' Job {j[\"id\"]}: {j[\"status\"]}') " sleep 60 done
task.verification: All jobs reach RUNNING status within 30 minutes. No persistent PENDING state (which would indicate us-central1 capacity issues).
task.rollback: If jobs stuck PENDING > 30 min: This is unlikely in us-central1 (highest global GPU inventory). If it happens: escalate to user, consider us-east4 or us-west1.

phase.name: Post-Migration Cleanup
phase.description: Clean up stale resources and update documentation. Only execute AFTER verifying experiments run successfully in us-central1.
phase.depends-on: Phase 6 (at least one experiment SUCCEEDED in us-central1)

task.title: Verify no europe-west4 resources remain
task.commands:
# Check for any lingering europe-west4 resources gcloud artifacts repositories list --project=minivess-mlops --location=europe-west4 gcloud sql instances list --project=minivess-mlops --format="table(name,region)" gcloud run services list --project=minivess-mlops --format="table(name,region)" gcloud compute instances list --project=minivess-mlops --format="table(name,zone,status)"
# Expected: ZERO resources in europe-west4 or europe-west1 # Only resources should be in us-central1
task.verification: Zero resources in any European region. All resources in us-central1.
task.rollback: If stale resources found: delete manually via gcloud. These are orphaned and cost money even when idle.

task.title: Update FinOps snapshot for us-central1
task.changes: Create a new FinOps snapshot documenting the us-central1 infrastructure state. Same format as gcp-finops-snapshot-2026-03-28-report.md but for new region.

task.title: Create metalearning document for this migration
task.content:
# Metalearning: Second Region Migration (europe-west4 -> us-central1)
**Date**: 2026-03-28 **Type**: Infrastructure migration **Duration**: ~3-4 hours **Reason**: GPU capacity exhaustion in europe-west4 for 13+ hours
## What Happened Second region migration in 2 days. europe-west4 L4 GPUs were fully exhausted across all 3 zones for 13+ hours, blocking all factorial experiments.
## Key Lessons 1. EU regions have LIMITED GPU capacity — 3 zones is not enough for guaranteed availability 2. us-central1 (Iowa) has 3 L4 zones + 4 A100 zones = highest global GPU availability 3. Clean-slate migration is fast when GCS buckets are empty 4. Docker image upload is the bottleneck (~30-60 min for 6 GB on residential internet) 5. The Pulumi code was already parameterized from the first migration — changing region was a config change, not a code change
## Prevention for Future - Multi-region config with us-central1 as primary, other US regions as fallback - Consider multi-region GAR repository for Docker image availability - Monitor GPU availability before launching large factorial experiments

phase.name: Cloud Test Suite Improvements
phase.description: Add deterministic tests that prevent future region drift and catch migration issues. These tests run as part of make test-staging and act as guardrails.

task.title: Test: all config files reference the same GCP region
task.description: Read GCP_REGION from .env.example, configs/registry/gar.yaml (server prefix), configs/cloud/gcp_spot.yaml (region field), and deployment/pulumi/gcp/Pulumi.yaml (default). Assert all four agree. Prevents region drift where one file gets updated and others are forgotten.

task.title: Test: GAR URL in SkyPilot YAMLs matches gar.yaml config
task.description: Read image_prefix from configs/registry/gar.yaml. For each SkyPilot YAML in deployment/skypilot/*.yaml, verify image_id starts with "docker:{image_prefix}". Prevents stale GAR URLs after region migration.

task.title: Test: .sky.yaml controller region matches gcp_spot.yaml region
task.description: Controller must be co-located with job region. Read gcp_spot.yaml region and .sky.yaml infra field. Assert they reference the same region.

task.title: Test: preflight_gcp.py GAR_IMAGE matches gar.yaml config
task.description: Read GAR_IMAGE from preflight_gcp.py (via AST parse, not regex per Rule 16). Read image_prefix from gar.yaml. Assert GAR_IMAGE starts with image_prefix. Prevents stale hardcoded URLs in the preflight script.

task.title: Test: region config files only reference the active region for co-located configs
task.description: For "strict" and "a100_fallback" region configs (the co-located configs), verify all region references match the configured GCP_REGION. Non-co-located configs (europe_us.yaml, world.yaml) are exempt — they intentionally reference multiple regions.

task.title: Test: Pulumi default region matches .env.example GCP_REGION
task.description: Read Pulumi.yaml default region and .env.example GCP_REGION. Assert they match. Prevents Pulumi deploying to a different region than the configs expect.

task.title: Test: knowledge graph cloud.yaml region matches active config
task.description: The KG cloud.yaml has a gcp.region field. Assert it matches the active region from configs/registry/gar.yaml. Prevents KG staleness.

verification.gate: All T0.* tasks pass. us-central1 has GPU quota, Docker images cached locally, DVC data cached locally.
Pulumi stack outputs show us-central1 for all resources. GAR images pushed. GCS buckets have DVC data. Cloud Run MLflow responds.
Zero active files reference europe-west4 (grep returns no matches). All config files reference us-central1 consistently.
make test-staging passes with 0 failures. preflight_gcp.py passes all checks. validate_yaml_contract.py passes. Dry-run factorial shows us-central1 references.
Single DynUNet job provisions, runs, and completes in us-central1. Docker pull time < 60s (same-region). Controller in us-central1.
Phase 1 validation jobs provision in us-central1 within 10 minutes. No 13-hour PENDING state (the problem this migration solves).

rollback.strategy:
If us-central1 migration fails at any phase, the rollback depends on how far we progressed. europe-west4 resources are destroyed in Phase 1, so rollback after Phase 1 means re-creating in europe-west4 (not restoring).
However: europe-west4 is GPU-EXHAUSTED. Rolling back to europe-west4 means returning to the 13+ hour PENDING state. There is no working state to roll back to. The only "rollback" is trying a different US region (us-east4, us-west1).

phases.phase-rollback: No changes made. Simply stop migration.
If pulumi destroy succeeded but pulumi up failed: 1. Fix the issue (quota, API, etc.) 2. Re-run pulumi up 3. Or: change region to us-east4 in Pulumi config and try there

git checkout -- . to revert all file changes. But infrastructure is already in us-central1 — config would be inconsistent. Must also revert Pulumi config and re-deploy to europe-west4 (not recommended).
If tests fail or smoke test fails: fix the issue. Do not revert infrastructure. The problem is likely a stale reference, not a fundamental migration issue.

cost-projection.current-monthly: GAR storage: EUR 0.57 GCS storage: EUR 0.00 (empty buckets) Cloud SQL: EUR 23.50 Cloud Run: EUR 2-6 SkyPilot Controller: EUR 93-100 (n4-standard-4, europe-west1-b) GPU compute: EUR 0 (cannot provision — capacity exhausted) Total: EUR 119-130/month (ZERO productive training)
cost-projection.projected-monthly: GAR storage: ~EUR 0.57 (us-central1 pricing similar) GCS storage: ~EUR 0.10-0.70 (once data is pushed) Cloud SQL: ~EUR 23.50 (same tier, similar pricing) Cloud Run: ~EUR 2-6 (similar) SkyPilot Controller: ~EUR 25-35 (e2-medium in us-central1, from .sky.yaml cpus: 2+) GPU compute (L4 spot): ~$0.224/hr (34% cheaper than europe-west4's $0.340/hr) Total always-on: ~EUR 52-66/month (controller downsized saves ~EUR 60/month) Per training job: ~EUR 0.80 (5h x $0.224/hr, 20% cheaper than europe-west4)
cost-projection.savings: Controller downsize: EUR 60-70/month savings L4 spot price: 34% cheaper ($0.224 vs $0.340/hr) GPU availability: us-central1 has 3 L4 zones + 4 A100 zones (highest globally) No more 13h PENDING waste: saves EUR 4.10/day of idle infrastructure per incident Cross-region egress: EUR 0 (same-region, identical to europe-west4 pattern)

plan.design-principle:
After this migration, changing region AGAIN requires: 1. Edit deployment/pulumi/gcp/Pulumi.yaml: default region 2. Edit deployment/pulumi/gcp/Pulumi.dev.yaml: gcp:region 3. Edit deployment/pulumi/gcp/__main__.py: default fallback + comments 4. Edit configs/registry/gar.yaml: server + image_prefix 5. Edit configs/cloud/gcp_spot.yaml: region + docker_image 6. Edit .sky.yaml: controller infra 7. Edit .env.example + .env: GCP_REGION + MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI 8. Create new configs/cloud/regions/{region}_strict.yaml 9. Update configs/factorial/debug.yaml: region_config 10. Update deployment/skypilot/*.yaml: image_id 11. Update scripts/preflight_gcp.py: GAR_IMAGE 12. Update CLAUDE.md + deployment/pulumi/gcp/CLAUDE.md 13. Update knowledge-graph/domains/cloud.yaml 14. Update .claude/rules/two-providers-only.md 15. Run: pulumi destroy + pulumi up 16. Push Docker images to new GAR 17. Push DVC data to new GCS bucket 18. Run: make test-staging (with Phase 8 tests, auto-catches drift)
Total effort: ~2-3 hours (Pulumi + Docker push + config + tests). Phase 8 tests catch region drift automatically — no manual grep needed.
